title: How much money does it cost to get started in aesthetics？
filename: How much money does it cost to get started in aesthetics？.en.vtt
excerpt: How much money does it cost to get started in medical aesthetics so in an unregulated market the cost can actually be very low but the cost to actually become proficient is much higher than most people think because it's very easy to attend a day and watch over someone's shoulder…

segments.text: how much money does it cost to get started in medical aesthetics so in an unregulated market the cost can actually be very low but the cost to actually become proficient is much higher than most people think because it's very easy to attend a day and watch over someone's shoulder while other people inject maybe you do one or two injections yourself you get your certificate but you're not actually confident and this is where the real cost starts to emerge it's one of the reasons we've always kept groups very small as can be training but even so it's usually only the start of a journey i would say the average practitioner by the time they are proficient has probably spent between five thousand and twelve thousand pounds on training the next thing is your venue where you actually practice now there are lots of different models for finding somewhere to work you can work in someone else's practice where you only pay for an hour in that particular room this is common everywhere from harley street to local beauty salons where you pay between 40 and 50 pounds for a slot only when you need it and you pay 40 pounds no matter what for the use of that room on that particular day and

then the more expensive option or the more risky option would be to book a room full time so you rent an entire building that will cost depending on where you are anything from 800 pounds to tens of thousands of pounds i'm imagining most newbies will be starting around 1200 pounds a month for a room in a city like manchester and this is high risk because you won't have the patience yet to fill it so i don't recommend unless you have a very solid marketing strategy that you start off with a big overhead find a way to get patients to get known in the area before you take on the big risks such as rent

transcript: how much money does it cost to get started in medical aesthetics so in an unregulated market the cost can actually be very low but the cost to actually become proficient is much higher than most people think because it's very easy to attend a day and watch over someone's shoulder while other people inject maybe you do one or two injections yourself you get your certificate but you're not actually confident and this is where the real cost starts to emerge it's one of the reasons we've always kept groups very small as can be training but even so it's usually only the start of a journey i would say the average practitioner by the time they are proficient has probably spent between five thousand and twelve thousand pounds on training the next thing is your venue where you actually practice now there are lots of different models for finding somewhere to work you can work in someone else's practice where you only pay for an hour in that particular room this is common everywhere from harley street to local beauty salons where you pay between 40 and 50 pounds for a slot only when you need it and you pay 40 pounds no matter what for the use of that room on that particular day and then the more expensive option or the more risky option would be to book a room full time so you rent an entire building that will cost depending on where you are anything from 800 pounds to tens of thousands of pounds i'm imagining most newbies will be starting around 1200 pounds a month for a room in a city like manchester and this is high risk because you won't have the patience yet to fill it so i don't recommend unless you have a very solid marketing strategy that you start off with a big overhead find a way to get patients to get known in the area before you take on the big risks such as rent
